topic: High earners cut obligations they don't want from their schedule.
title: The One Thing Millionaires Cut From Their Schedule

scenes.setting: A modern, clean office with a desk and a laptop, natural light from a window.
scenes.scene_edit_prompt: Keep the same person, face, head position, lighting on the face, and expression EXACTLY the same. Only change the background and environment behind them. Make it a modern office with a desk and a laptop, natural light from a window.
scenes.spoken_text: Stop saying yes to obligations. I surveyed 100 people earning over a million a year.

scenes.setting: A minimalist living room with a couch and a bookshelf, soft lighting.
scenes.scene_edit_prompt: Keep the same person, face, head position, lighting on the face, and expression EXACTLY the same. Only change the background and environment behind them. Make it a minimalist living room with a couch and a bookshelf, soft lighting.
scenes.spoken_text: Their answer was almost identical. They cut every commitment they didn't truly want.

scenes.setting: A quiet coffee shop with a wooden table and a latte, warm ambient lighting.
scenes.scene_edit_prompt: Keep the same person, face, head position, lighting on the face, and expression EXACTLY the same. Only change the background and environment behind them. Make it a quiet coffee shop with a wooden table and a latte, warm ambient lighting.
scenes.spoken_text: No networking events they dreaded. No meetings that drained them. Just hard no's.

scenes.setting: A rooftop terrace with a city skyline in the background, golden hour light.
scenes.scene_edit_prompt: Keep the same person, face, head position, lighting on the face, and expression EXACTLY the same. Only change the background and environment behind them. Make it a rooftop terrace with a city skyline in the background, golden hour light.
scenes.spoken_text: So I learned to stop saying yes to obligations. That's the real secret.
